goal: Mission Control can be started from one canonical operator command that refreshes current runtime visibility artifacts before serving the viewer.
risk: Operators and AI keep treating Mission Control as a manual wiring step, so status visibility does not appear during runtime operation.
loss_boundary: Do not claim Mission Control auto-start unless a first-class CLI command updates packet artifacts and launches a viewer session path with test coverage.
acceptance_gates: mission-control command is registered in CLI catalog, command registry, docs, and quickstart
missionControlCommand regression test proves packet refresh, viewer session, and browser-open path
runtime audits and npm test pass after TASK-087
evidence_plan: CLI wrapper, registry entries, docs, runtime-core-2 regression, session observability audit, release-state audit
stop_conditions: Stop if the command cannot reuse the existing visibility-session path without making read commands long-running.
Stop if tests show viewer startup breaks existing visibility-session behavior.
